basic_details.job_title: Cordite Factory Aruvankadu Trade Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 47 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Cordite Factory, Aruvankadu (CFA), a unit of Munitions India Limited under the Ministry of Defence, has released HRD Advertisement No. 1457 dated 11.07.2026, inviting online-form-download/offline applications from Indian citizens for engagement as Trade Apprentices (Non-ITI & ITI candidates) for the 59th Batch under the Trade Apprentices Act, 1961. This article summarises the official notification in full, based strictly on the details published by CFA. Where the notification does not mention a particular detail, this article states that clearly instead of assuming it.

application_fee.details: The official notification does not mention any application fee. No fee amount, payment mode, or fee exemption details are specified in the source document, so this information is not available.

age_limit.details: Minimum age: 18 years for designated trades related to hazardous industries, as per Section 3(a) of the Apprentices Act, 1961, and amendments thereof. Maximum age: 35 years for all categories, as per Clause 3 of the National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme-2 (NAPS-2) issued by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship vide MSDE-5/01/2022-AP dated 25.08.2023.

qualification.details: Minimum age: 18 years for designated trades related to hazardous industries, as per Section 3(a) of the Apprentices Act, 1961, and amendments thereof. Maximum age: 35 years for all categories, as per Clause 3 of the National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme-2 (NAPS-2) issued by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship vide MSDE-5/01/2022-AP dated 25.08.2023.

vacancy_details.Trade: Ex ITI Attendant Operator Chemical Plant

category_wise_breakdown.trade: Ex ITI Attendant Operator Chemical Plant

salary_details.details: As per Rule 11(1) of the Apprenticeship Rules, 1992, and Gazette Notification G.S.R. 610(E) dated 03.09.2025 (Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship, Letter No. DGT-36/1/2021-AP dated 15.10.2025), the monthly stipend is as follows: | No other allowance is paid during the entire apprenticeship training period. For Ex-ITI candidates, the stipend consideration also accounts for any apprenticeship period already undergone. The stipend amount is subject to change based on future government notifications.

selection_process: Grade Marks Range Percentage
Selection is made purely on the basis of merit
with separate merit lists prepared for the Non-ITI and Ex-ITI categories.
Non-ITI merit list: Based on the overall average percentage of marks obtained in Madhyamik/Matriculation (Class X or equivalent) - either aggregate of all subjects or the best 5 subjects
as per the concerned Board's criteria. Ex-ITI merit list: Based on the simple average of percentage of marks in both Matriculation and ITI (minimum 50% aggregate required in each).
Where a candidate's Class X result is declared in CGPA/GPA
marks must be entered after conversion as per the concerned Board's own criteria (not the CGPA grade itself). If the Board has not issued a clear conversion formula
or if the candidate does not know it

a standard multiplication factor of 9.5 is applied uniformly. Using an incorrect conversion formula (different from the one mandated by the Board) will lead to rejection at the document verification stage.
The older candidate (higher age) is preferred. If the date of birth is also the same
the candidate holding a Matriculation certificate of an earlier passing year is preferred. If the year of passing Matriculation is also the same
the candidate whose name comes first in English alphabetical order is preferred.
Based on the vacancy position
select lists are prepared

and only provisionally selected candidates are called for document verification and medical examination. CFA Management reserves the right to revise the select list at any time in case of any error or oversight
and its decision is final.

physical_eligibility.details: Physical fitness standards follow the Apprentices Act, 1961, and Apprenticeship Rules, 1992. After successful document verification, provisionally selected candidates undergo a medical examination at Cordite Factory premises. The date, time, and venue will be communicated separately by the factory. Only candidates who pass the medical examination proceed to Trade Apprentice training. Medical fitness is certified by the Cordite Factory Hospital.

how_to_apply: Download the Application Form using the link provided on the official website and take a printout.
Fill the form in BLOCK LETTERS only, entering personal details carefully. Name, father's name, date of birth, and marks must exactly match your Matriculation/equivalent certificate.
Attach all required enclosures, including one extra self-attested photograph (signed on the back).
Superscribe the envelope as: "APPLICATION FOR THE INDUCTION OF TRADE APPRENTICES - (ATTENDANT OPERATOR CHEMICAL PLANT)" as printed in the notification.
Send the completed application by post to the address below.
The Chief General Manager, Cordite Factory, Aruvankadu, The Nilgiris District, Tamil Nadu - 643202

The notification advises candidates to keep their mobile number and email ID active throughout the recruitment process, as all important communication will be sent via email/SMS and will be deemed to have been read by the candidate.
important_instructions: As per the official notification, candidates who have already undergone apprenticeship and possess a National Apprentice Certificate (NAC) are not eligible to apply for this recruitment.

Submission of the application form does not guarantee selection as a Trade Apprentice; selection depends on the merit/select list and available vacancies. Candidature remains provisional at all stages until document scrutiny, police verification, medical examination, and other eligibility checks are completed. However, a pending or delayed police verification report will not be grounds for rejecting an otherwise eligible candidate. Candidates must regularly check the official website (https://www.ddpdoo.gov.in) for updates and changes. Canvassing of any kind will lead to disqualification. Suppression of material facts or submission of forged certificates will lead to rejection of candidature at any stage. CFA's decision on eligibility and acceptance/rejection of any application is final and binding; no correspondence will be entertained. Any dispute relating to this recruitment is subject to the jurisdiction of courts/tribunals covering the city/town where Cordite Factory is situated.

rows.details: Cordite Factory, Aruvankadu (CFA), a unit of Munitions India Limited, Govt. of India Enterprise, Ministry of Defence
Application form to be downloaded, printed, filled in BLOCK LETTERS, and sent by post

content_sections.heading: Important Note Before You Apply
content_sections.text: As per the official notification, candidates who have already undergone apprenticeship and possess a National Apprentice Certificate (NAC) are not eligible to apply for this recruitment.

content_sections.heading: CFA Aruvankadu Recruitment 2026: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates are advised in the notification to submit their applications well before the closing date to avoid last-minute rush. CFA states it will not take responsibility for candidates who are unable to submit their applications by the last day for any reason.

content_sections.heading: CFA Aruvankadu Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: The total number of vacancies is 47, described in the notification as 20 Non-ITI, 20 Ex-ITI, and 7 Ex-ITI Laboratory posts. The notification also provides a detailed, category-wise vacancy table (Annexure-1) for Cordite Factory, Aruvankadu, Tamil Nadu, reproduced below exactly as published: Note on the vacancy table: The official annexure labels all three trades with the prefix "Ex ITI" in the printed table, even though the introductory paragraph of the notification separately describes the 47 vacancies as 20 Non-ITI, 20 Ex-ITI, and 7 Ex-ITI Laboratory. This is exactly how the figures appear in the source document; candidates should verify the applicable trade and category against the official PDF/website before applying. The notification also clarifies that the notified vacancy numbers are provisional, and PH/PWD candidates may apply against available vacancies as per their eligibility, with reservation applied within the total vacancies (not as a separate quota) as per applicable rules.

rows.trade: Ex ITI Attendant Operator Chemical Plant

content_sections.heading: CFA Aruvankadu Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Minimum age: 18 years for designated trades related to hazardous industries, as per Section 3(a) of the Apprentices Act, 1961, and amendments thereof. Maximum age: 35 years for all categories, as per Clause 3 of the National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme-2 (NAPS-2) issued by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship vide MSDE-5/01/2022-AP dated 25.08.2023.

rows.qualification_required: Passed Madhyamik (Class X standard or equivalent) as on the closing date of application, with a minimum of 50% marks as per the concerned Board's criteria (as per original mark sheet), and a minimum of 40% marks in both Mathematics and Science.

1) Passed the relevant trade test from an institute recognised by NCVT or SCVT (or any other authority notified by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship / Ministry of Labour and Employment) with a duration as per the Trade Apprentices Act, 1961, securing a minimum of 50% marks in the ITI examination. The relevant trade is determined strictly on the basis of the Schedule to the Apprentices Act, 1961. 2) Must have also passed Madhyamik/Class X or equivalent with a minimum of 50% marks.

content_sections.heading: CFA Aruvankadu Stipend / Salary 2026
content_sections.text: As per Rule 11(1) of the Apprenticeship Rules, 1992, and Gazette Notification G.S.R. 610(E) dated 03.09.2025 (Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship, Letter No. DGT-36/1/2021-AP dated 15.10.2025), the monthly stipend is as follows: No other allowance is paid during the entire apprenticeship training period. For Ex-ITI candidates, the stipend consideration also accounts for any apprenticeship period already undergone. The stipend amount is subject to change based on future government notifications.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The official notification does not mention any application fee. No fee amount, payment mode, or fee exemption details are specified in the source document, so this information is not available.

content_sections.heading: CFA Aruvankadu Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Selection is made purely on the basis of merit, with separate merit lists prepared for the Non-ITI and Ex-ITI categories. Non-ITI merit list: Based on the overall average percentage of marks obtained in Madhyamik/Matriculation (Class X or equivalent) - either aggregate of all subjects or the best 5 subjects, as per the concerned Board's criteria. Ex-ITI merit list: Based on the simple average of percentage of marks in both Matriculation and ITI (minimum 50% aggregate required in each). Where a candidate's Class X result is declared in CGPA/GPA, marks must be entered after conversion as per the concerned Board's own criteria (not the CGPA grade itself). If the Board has not issued a clear conversion formula, or if the candidate does not know it, a standard multiplication factor of 9.5 is applied uniformly. Using an incorrect conversion formula (different from the one mandated by the Board) will lead to rejection at the document verification stage. The older candidate (higher age) is preferred. If the date of birth is also the same, the candidate holding a Matriculation certificate of an earlier passing year is preferred. If the year of passing Matriculation is also the same, the candidate whose name comes first in English alphabetical order is preferred. Based on the vacancy position, select lists are prepared, and only provisionally selected candidates are called for document verification and medical examination. CFA Management reserves the right to revise the select list at any time in case of any error or oversight, and its decision is final.

content_sections.heading: Reservation for SC/ST/OBC and PH/PWD Candidates
content_sections.text: Reservation applies as per the relevant schedule under the Apprentices Act, 1961, the Apprenticeship Rules, 1992, the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, and existing Government of India rules. Vacancies for PH/PWD candidates are not separate but are included within the total notified vacancies. If the prescribed number of SC or ST candidates is not available, unfilled seats are transferred as per the standard inter-category rules described in the notification (SC/ST interchange, and OBC seats going to non-OBC candidates if unfilled).

content_sections.heading: Duration of Training
content_sections.text: Non-ITI and ITI candidates: As specified under the Trade Apprentices Act, 1961, and Apprenticeship Rules, 1992, and amendments thereof. Ex-ITI candidates: As per Schedule I of the Apprenticeship Rules, 1992, and amendments thereof, for the respective trade.

content_sections.heading: Medical Examination and Physical Fitness
content_sections.text: Physical fitness standards follow the Apprentices Act, 1961, and Apprenticeship Rules, 1992. After successful document verification, provisionally selected candidates undergo a medical examination at Cordite Factory premises. The date, time, and venue will be communicated separately by the factory. Only candidates who pass the medical examination proceed to Trade Apprentice training. Medical fitness is certified by the Cordite Factory Hospital.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for CFA Aruvankadu Application
content_sections.text: Recent clear colour photograph (3.5 cm x 3.5 cm), not older than three months from the date of publication of the notification, plus one extra self-attested photograph (signed on the back) Clear scanned signature on white paper in black/blue ink (3.5 cm x 3.5 cm) 12-digit Aadhaar Card number, or the first 14-digit Aadhaar Enrolment ID (if Aadhaar is not yet issued) Identity Card copy (such as Voter ID), if Aadhaar is unavailable SSC/Matriculation (Class X) or equivalent mark sheet Consolidated mark sheet for all semesters of the relevant ITI pass trade, or Provisional National Trade Certificate showing marks National Trade Certificate (NCVT) or Provisional National Trade Certificate (NCVT/SCVT) for ITI-pass candidates Caste certificate for SC/ST/OBC candidates, wherever applicable; OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates must also submit their non-creamy layer certificate issued within the last three years Physical Disability certificate for PH/PWD candidates, issued by a competent Medical Board/Authority This provision applies to candidates from all States and Union Territories except Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh, Meghalaya, and Assam. Eligible candidates from other states/UTs may submit any one of the following as ID proof: Voter ID Card Passport Driving Licence Photo ID card issued by Central/State Government Student Identity Card with photograph issued by a recognised school/college Photo-bearing nationalised bank passbook Bank passbook Voter Identity Card Passport Latest electricity bill Latest telephone bill Driving Licence Property tax receipt Aadhaar Card

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for CFA Aruvankadu Trade Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Download the Application Form using the link provided on the official website and take a printout. Fill the form in BLOCK LETTERS only, entering personal details carefully. Name, father's name, date of birth, and marks must exactly match your Matriculation/equivalent certificate. Attach all required enclosures, including one extra self-attested photograph (signed on the back). Superscribe the envelope as: "APPLICATION FOR THE INDUCTION OF TRADE APPRENTICES - (ATTENDANT OPERATOR CHEMICAL PLANT)" as printed in the notification. Send the completed application by post to the address below. The Chief General Manager, Cordite Factory, Aruvankadu, The Nilgiris District, Tamil Nadu - 643202 The notification advises candidates to keep their mobile number and email ID active throughout the recruitment process, as all important communication will be sent via email/SMS and will be deemed to have been read by the candidate.

content_sections.heading: Terms and Conditions of Engagement
content_sections.text: Selected candidates must execute a Contract of Apprenticeship (by the guardian, if the candidate is a minor). During training, apprentices are entitled to medical treatment only from the Cordite Factory Hospital, as per rules. Training is subject to registration with the respective RDSDE (Regional Directorate of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship). If registration is not granted for any reason, the candidate's training will be terminated immediately. On completing training and passing the NCVT-conducted test, a Certificate of Proficiency in the concerned trade is issued. If a candidate's Contract of Apprenticeship is prematurely terminated due to non-compliance with contract terms, the candidate/surety must pay the training cost amount determined by the Apprenticeship Adviser. There is no obligation on CFA to offer employment after training, nor any obligation on the apprentice to accept an offer if made. No Transport Allowance is payable to candidates called for document verification or medical examination.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions for Candidates
content_sections.text: Submission of the application form does not guarantee selection as a Trade Apprentice; selection depends on the merit/select list and available vacancies. Candidature remains provisional at all stages until document scrutiny, police verification, medical examination, and other eligibility checks are completed. However, a pending or delayed police verification report will not be grounds for rejecting an otherwise eligible candidate. Candidates must regularly check the official website (https://www.ddpdoo.gov.in) for updates and changes. Canvassing of any kind will lead to disqualification. Suppression of material facts or submission of forged certificates will lead to rejection of candidature at any stage. CFA's decision on eligibility and acceptance/rejection of any application is final and binding; no correspondence will be entertained. Any dispute relating to this recruitment is subject to the jurisdiction of courts/tribunals covering the city/town where Cordite Factory is situated.

content_sections.heading: Grounds for Rejection of Candidature
content_sections.text: As per the notification, an application/candidature may be rejected for reasons including:

rows.details: The Chief General Manager, Cordite Factory, Aruvankadu, The Nilgiris District, Tamil Nadu - 643202

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the CFA Trade Apprentice posts?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the offline application for the CFA Trade Apprentice posts is 31st July 2026. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well in advance to avoid any last-minute issues.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for CFA Trade Apprentice recruitment?
faq.answer: For Non-ITI candidates, Madhyamik (Class X) with a minimum of 50% marks and 40% in Mathematics and Science is required. For Ex-ITI candidates, ITI Pass in a relevant trade with a minimum of 50% marks and Matriculation with a minimum of 50% marks is necessary.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for applying to CFA Trade Apprentice posts?
faq.answer: The minimum age limit for applying is 18 years, and the maximum age limit is 35 years. Age relaxation will be provided as per government norms.

faq.question: Q4. How many vacancies are available for the Trade Apprentice posts at CFA?
faq.answer: There are a total of 47 vacancies for the Trade Apprentice posts at Cordite Factory Aruvankadu (CFA).

faq.question: Q5. What is the application mode for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application mode for this recruitment is offline. Candidates need to download the application form and submit it via post or as specified in the notification.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary offered for the Trade Apprentice positions?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will receive a monthly stipend ranging from Rs. 8200 to Rs. 9600 per month.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for CFA Trade Apprentice recruitment?
faq.answer: The selection process includes the preparation of a merit list based on the marks obtained by the candidates, followed by document verification and a medical examination.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any application fee for the CFA Trade Apprentice posts?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for the CFA Trade Apprentice posts.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I download the official notification and application form?
faq.answer: The official notification and application form can be downloaded from the official website of Cordite Factory Aruvankadu, which is https://www.ddpdoo.gov.in.

faq.question: Q10. What is the job type for these Trade Apprentice positions?
faq.answer: The job type for these Trade Apprentice positions is contractual.
